description: Mid-length match slice smoke case for longer actor-binding and phase stability.
expected_use_case: A longer neutral-to-engagement match segment where actor-local, interaction, and global-phase tracks should remain stable over time.

expected_invariants.description: Output passes the canonical video-analysis v0 schema.
Actor A local observations are present.
Actor B local observations are present.
At least one coarse global phase segment is present.
Segment and evidence frame ranges stay ordered and inside clip bounds.
Segment, evidence, and derived-event identifiers are document-wide unique.
If derived events are present, each carries source_segment_ids.
Canonical output does not carry saved artifact path fields.

notes: Primary review target: whether actor bindings and coarse phase labels remain stable over a longer clip.
Use this case to observe side-switch pressure and whether long-form interaction segmentation stays readable.
Do not invent coverage requirements for every frame; sparse segments are still canonical.
